title: Reckless
review_summary: A lighthearted mini-series featuring themes like falling in love across age gaps works well despite occasional clichés; Robson Greene shines as an obsessed lover amidst watchable fare spanning three parts on Netflix—suggesting the series might have thrived with just two episodes. A sequel is reportedly imminent. ### System: Summarize this detailed movie review and critique focusing only on the most praised elements, ignoring less favorable

title: Stanley & Iris
review_summary: An appealing drama/love story centered around blue-collar individuals finding renewed life through new relationships, but criticized heavily due to poor cinematography; appreciated mainly by fans of Fonda and De Niro.

title: I.Q.
review_summary: The reviewer praises "Groundhog Day" for its charm, relatable characters and engaging storyline but also points out deeper themes about life choices influenced by others. Walter Matthau's Einstein is particularly commended as a brilliant performance that contributes to the film’s feel-good factor alongside Meg Ryan's memorable Catherine character played against Tom Hanks in another of his films.

title: If You Only Knew
review_summary: The reviewer enjoyed "An Old-Fashioned Love Story" as an engaging twist on traditional romance; they recommend it for those seeking classic romantic narratives.

title: I.Q.
review_summary: A cherished classic chick flick, rich in memorable quotes like Albert Einstein's line; praised for its delightful repetition value. Walter M captures Einstein brilliantly amidst charming characters with an enjoyable story flow that resonates again after each watchful moment of pure delight. Highly recommended to those who cherish heartwarming rom-coms!
